children.heading: Investigation by General Counsel; issuance of complaint; statement of reasons
children.label: a Investigation by General Counsel; issuance of complaint; statement of reasons
children.text: If the Department or labor organization is charged by any person with having engaged in or engaging in an unfair labor practice, the General Counsel shall investigate the charge and may issue and cause to be served upon the Department or labor organization a complaint. In any case in which the General Counsel does not issue a complaint because the charge fails to state an unfair labor practice, the General Counsel shall provide the person making the charge a written statement of the reasons for not issuing a complaint.
that a hearing will be held before the Board (or any member thereof or before an individual employed by the Board and designated for such purpose); and
of the time and place fixed for the hearing.

children.heading: Answer; personal appearance
children.label: c Answer; personal appearance
children.text: The labor organization or Department involved shall have the right to file an answer to the original and any amended complaint and to appear in person or otherwise and give testimony at the time and place fixed in the complaint for the hearing.

children.heading: Time of filing of charges
children.label: d Time of filing of charges

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no complaint shall be issued based on any alleged unfair labor practice which occurred more than 6 months before the filing of the charge with the Board.

children.label: A
children.text: any failure of the Department or labor organization against which the charge is made to perform a duty owed to the person, or

children.label: B
children.text: any concealment which prevented discovery of the alleged unfair labor practice during the 6-month period,

children.heading: Regulations providing for resolution through informal methods
children.label: e Regulations providing for resolution through informal methods
children.text: The General Counsel may prescribe regulations providing for informal methods by which the alleged unfair labor practice may be resolved prior to the issuance of a complaint.

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: f Hearing
children.text: The Board (or any member thereof or any individual employed by the Board and designated for such purpose) shall conduct a hearing on the complaint not earlier than 5 days after the date on which the complaint is served. In the discretion of the individual or individuals conducting the hearing, any person involved may be allowed to intervene in the hearing and to present testimony. Any such hearing shall, to the extent practicable, be conducted in accordance with the provisions of subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5, except that the parties shall not be bound by rules of evidence, whether statutory, common law, or adopted by a court. A transcript shall be kept of the hearing. After such a hearing the Board, in its discretion, may upon notice receive further evidence or hear argument.

children.heading: Findings of fact relative to issuance of orders; backpay
children.label: g Findings of fact relative to issuance of orders; backpay

children.text: to cease and desist from any such unfair labor practice in which the Department or labor organization is engaged;
requiring the parties to renegotiate a collective bargaining agreement in accordance with the order of the Board and requiring that the agreement, as amended, be given retroactive effect;
requiring reinstatement of an employee with backpay in accordance with ; orsection 5596 of title 5
including any combination of the actions described in paragraphs (1) through (3) or such other action as will carry out the purpose of this subchapter.

children.heading: Findings of fact requiring dismissal of complaint
children.label: h Findings of fact requiring dismissal of complaint
children.text: If the individual or individuals conducting the hearing determine that the preponderance of the evidence received fails to demonstrate that the Department or labor organization named in the complaint has engaged in or is engaging in an unfair labor practice, the individual or individuals shall state in writing their findings of fact and shall issue an order dismissing the complaint.
